textRun.content: This Agency Agreement (the “Agreement”) is between Hunter Hoffman (the "
") and EMPYREAN CREATIVE LLC, an Oregon Limited Liability Company (the "
") (all hereinafter collectively referred to as the “Parties”).
Principal is in the business of e-learning and clothing and wants to engage Agent as its exclusive provider of Marketing services.
Agent has performed the same or similar activities for others.
Agent shall perform a variety of services for Principal as provided in the attached in Exhibit A

(the "Service Estimate"). Principal’s proposed Service Estimate shall become legally binding upon payment of the deposit, electronically or in person. During the term of this Agreement, Principal may wish to add additional services beyond what has been outlined in the Service Estimate (“Add-Ons”). Parties must agree and put in writing any changes or Add-Ons to this Agreement.

Parties agree to hold each other's privileged and private information (“Confidential Information”) in strict confidence. Any information that is obtained regarding Principal will be treated as Confidential Information and will not be released or revealed to any person without Principal’s express written consent. Agent acknowledges that it and its contractors, employees, or representatives may, in the course of performance of this Agreement, be exposed to or acquire information that is proprietary to the Principal. Any and all information obtained by Agent or its officers, directors, employees, and representatives in the performance of this Agreement which relates to Principal shall be deemed to be part of the Principal’s Confidential Information for the purposes of this Agreement. Parties agree not to make each other's Confidential Information available in any form to any third party or to use each other's Confidential Information for any purpose other than as specified in this Agreement.

Parties agree to communicate efficiently, timely, and intentionally throughout this process. Should either party be unhappy with services related to this Agreement, prior to discussing the other party on social media, review platforms (ie. Yelp, Google, Facebook, NextDoor, Bing), or any other public platform, Parties agree to discuss the matter. And upon doing so, come to a resolution without defamation or defamation like communication.

(a) Term. This Agreement shall become effective as described in Section 1. Unless it is terminated earlier in accordance with subsection 2(b), this Agreement will continue until the services have been satisfactorily completed and Agent has been paid in full for those services (the "Term").
(i) by either party on provision of 30 days' written notice to the other party, with or without cause;

(ii) by Principal at any time and by either party for a material breach of any provision of this agreement by the other party, if the other party's material breach is not cured within 7 days of receipt of written notice of the breach; or

(iii) without prior notice, if Agent fails or refuses to comply with the written policies or reasonable directives of the Principal, or is guilty of serious misconduct in connection with performance under this agreement.

After the termination of this Agreement for any reason, Principal shall promptly pay Agent for services rendered before the effective date of the termination. No other compensation, of any nature or type, will be payable after the termination of this agreement.

Agent may not represent itself as having any powers except those specified in this agreement. Without limiting the foregoing, Agent does not have authority to Appointment setting, Lead Conversion or otherwise obligate Principal in any way except as stated in this Agreement or otherwise specifically authorized in writing by Principal.

Principal shall pay Agent according to the following schedule: An initial payment which will be stipulated in the Service Estimate, payable at the time of signing of this Agreement.  Then, Principal shall make a recurring monthly payment of $10,000 due on the 1st of each new month, and 10% revenue share from the campaigns for as long as this Agreement stipulates. Principal may make payments to Agent by credit card, debit card, or wire transfer. Principal shall make recurring monthly payments upon receipt of monthly invoice, with a twenty-four-hour grace period; after which a non-compounding late fee of 5.0% accrues weekly on the outstanding amount.

If a dispute arises out of or relates to this Agreement, or the alleged breach thereof, and if the dispute is not settled through communication and negotiation, Parties agree first to try in good faith to settle the dispute by mediation within 60 days of the raised issue before resorting to binding arbitration.  Parties shall appoint an agreed upon neutral third party with experience mediating business-related disputes.

(a) Agent solely responsible for taxes. Agent acknowledges that Agent is not Principal's employee. Furthermore, Agent is solely responsible for reporting and paying any tax or other cost assessed on the basis of Principal's payment of compensation to Agent under this Agreement.

Principal shall not withhold any amount of compensation for the Agent's taxes, including income tax, social security and Medicare tax, workers' compensation taxes or costs, unemployment compensation taxes or costs, or any other tax, cost, fee, or charge related to Agent's compensation for services under this Agreement.

Parties subject to this Agreement understand and acknowledge that this Agreement is not exclusive. Each Party respectively agrees that they are free to enter into other similar Agreements with other parties.

Principal and Agent agree that Agent shall perform its duties under this Agreement as an independent contractor.  In no way does this Agreement become or suggest a business partnership, joint venture, or employer/employee relationship. Agent and Principal shall not act or represent or hold itself out as having authority to act as an agent or partner of the other or in any way bind or commit the other to any obligations, without the prior written consent of the other.

Materials produced or developed under this Agreement, including but not limited to, moral rights, goodwill, trade secrets, applications for registrations or relevant registration, rights to any trademark, patent, copyright, trade name, and industrial design are referred to as (“Intellectual Property”). Agent understands that the relationship of this Agreement is “work for hire,” and Intellectual Property shall be the sole property of the Principal. Principal’s use of the Intellectual Property shall not be restricted in any manner.

Principal acknowledges and hereby consents to the Service Estimate from Agent. Principal acknowledges that Agent has made no promises or guarantees about the outcome or result of services, and nothing in this Agreement or in the products purchased or delivered shall be construed as such. Agent, its members, managers, employees, contractors and agents will make reasonable efforts to minimize these risks. Agent hereby disclaims any and all liability arising from services rendered to Principal. Principal hereby releases, covenants not to sue, discharges, and holds harmless Agent, their employees, contractors, agents, members, managers and representatives, of and from the Claims, including but not limited to all liabilities, claims, actions, damages, penalties, fines, legal fees, costs or expenses of any kind arising out of or relating thereto or any ORS or Executive Order requirements.

Principal covenants and agrees that Principal and its representatives or agents shall be responsible for the receipt, inspection and acceptance of the services at the time and Principal shall be estopped from later claiming any damage or imperfections to the services after completion. Principal hereby releases, discharges and waives any and all responsibility of Agent from and against any liability of injury, including death, and for damage to or loss of property which may be suffered by Principal arising out of, or in any way connected with Principal’s participation in the services. Principal agrees to indemnify, save and hold Agent harmless from and against all damages, liabilities, costs, losses or expenses arising out of any claim, demand, or action by a third party arising out of any breach of Principal’s responsibilities or obligations, representations or warranties under this Agreement. Under such circumstances, Principal shall promptly notify Agent in writing of any claim or suit. Agent has sole control of the defense and all related settlement negotiations. Principal shall provide Agent with commercially reasonable assistance, information and authority necessary to perform Principal’s obligations under this section. This subsection shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement.

The Parties both agree to take all necessary precautions to prevent injury to any persons or damage to property during the term of this Agreement, and shall indemnify, defend and hold harmless the other Party, its officers, directors, shareholders, employees, representatives and/or agents from any claim, liability, loss, cost, damage, judgment, settlement or expense (including attorney’s fees) resulting from or arising in any way out of injury (including death) to any person or damage to property arising in any way out of any act, error, omission or negligence on the other Party and the other Party’s employees in the performance or failure to fulfill any services or obligations under this Agreement.

A party will not be considered in breach of or in default because of, and will not be liable to the other party for, any delay or failure to perform its obligations under this agreement by reason of fire, earthquake, flood, explosion, strike, riot, war, terrorism, pandemic, epidemic, or similar event beyond that party's reasonable control (each a "Force Majeure Event"). However, if a Force Majeure Event occurs, the affected party shall, as soon as practicable:
(a) notify the other party of the Force Majeure Event and its impact on performance under this Agreement; and
(b) use reasonable efforts to resolve any issues resulting from the Force Majeure Event and perform its obligations under this Agreement.

The laws of the state of Oregon govern this Agreement (without giving effect to its conflicts of law principles). Both parties consent to the personal jurisdiction of the state and federal courts in Washington County, Oregon.
No amendment to this Agreement will be effective unless it is in writing and signed by a party or its authorized representative.

No Assignment. Neither party may assign any of its rights under this Agreement, except with the prior written consent of the other party. All voluntary assignments of rights are limited by this subsection.
Parties may execute this Agreement in any number of counterparts, each of which is an original but all of which constitute one and the same instrument.

This Agreement, agreements ancillary to this Agreement, and related documents entered into in connection with this Agreement are signed when a party's signature is delivered by facsimile, email, or other electronic medium. These signatures must be treated in all respects as having the same force and effect as original signatures.

If any one or more of the provisions contained in this Agreement is, for any reason, held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable in any respect, that invalidity, illegality, or unenforceability will not affect any other provisions of this Agreement, but this Agreement will be construed as if those invalid, illegal, or unenforceable provisions had never been contained in it, unless the deletion of those provisions would result in such a material change so as to cause completion of the transactions contemplated by this agreement to be unreasonable.

(a) Writing; Permitted Delivery Methods. Each party giving or making any notice, request, demand, or other communication required or permitted by this agreement shall give that notice in writing and use one of the following types of delivery, each of which is a writing for purposes of this agreement: personal delivery, mail (registered or certified mail, postage prepaid, return-receipt requested), nationally recognized overnight courier (fees prepaid), facsimile, or email.
(b) Addresses. A party shall address notices under this section to a party at the following addresses:
Address: 2646 N Lambert St, Cornelius, Oregon 97113
A notice is effective only if the party giving notice complies with subsections (a) and (b) and if the recipient receives the notice.

No waiver of a breach, failure of any condition, or any right or remedy contained in or granted by the provisions of this agreement will be effective unless it is in writing and signed by the party waiving the breach, failure, right, or remedy. No waiver of any breach, failure, right, or remedy will be deemed a waiver of any other breach, failure, right, or remedy, whether or not similar, and no waiver will constitute a continuing waiver, unless the writing so specifies.

This Agreement constitutes the final agreement of the parties. It is the complete and exclusive expression of the parties' agreement about the subject matter of this Agreement. All prior and contemporaneous communications, negotiations, and agreements between the parties relating to the subject matter of this Agreement are expressly merged into and superseded by this Agreement. The provisions of this agreement may not be explained, supplemented, or qualified by evidence of trade usage or a prior course of dealings. Neither party was induced to enter this Agreement by, and neither party is relying on, any statement, representation, warranty, or agreement of the other party except those set forth expressly in this Agreement. Except as set forth expressly in this Agreement, there are no conditions precedent to this agreement's effectiveness.
The descriptive headings of the sections and subsections of this agreement are for convenience only, and do not affect this Agreement's construction or interpretation.

This Agreement will become effective when all parties have signed it. The date this Agreement is signed by the last party to sign it (as indicated by the date associated with that party's signature) will be deemed the date of this Agreement.

Each party shall use all reasonable efforts to take, or cause to be taken, all actions necessary or desirable to consummate and make effective the transactions this agreement contemplates or to evidence or carry out the intent and purposes of this Agreement.
Each party is signing this agreement on the date stated above that party's signature.

90-day Brand Scaler Program - A high-impact, 90-day program designed to accelerate brand growth through strategic marketing, content creation, and audience engagement. The program can be renewed or concluded after the initial term.
Campaign Strategy & Creation – Data-driven marketing strategy tailored for maximum brand growth.
Strategy Sessions – Ensuring alignment with audience insights and business objectives.
End-to-End Social Media Management – Full-service content planning, scheduling, and execution.
High-End Cinematic Editing – Professional post-production for visually compelling storytelling.
Seven Cowboys Brand Content – Authentic lifestyle-based storytelling for product-driven engagement.
Influencer & Collab Content Production – Expanding reach through strategic partnerships.
Audience Growth & Engagement Strategies – Building a loyal and engaged community.
Long-Form YouTube Content (4 videos/month) – Strengthening brand authority through in-depth storytelling.
Short-Form Video Content (16 videos/month) – Maximizing engagement on TikTok, Instagram, and YouTube.
Sales Funnel Creation – Designing high-converting funnels to drive leads and revenue.
Copywriting Strategy & Creation – Crafting persuasive messaging that resonates with your audience.
Campaign Monitoring & Scaling – Tracking performance, testing variables, and optimizing for results.
Performance Tracking & Optimization – Analyzing audience behavior and refining content strategy.
Biweekly Strategy Calls – 45-minute Zoom calls to review progress, adjust strategies, and discuss growth opportunities.
Weekly Video Update – A 3-15 minute Loom video summarizing ad performance and key insights.
Weekly Written Update – A concise Thursday update with key highlights from the past 3-4 days.
